backlog.title: Bug Fix Implementation — SIGTERM Teardown, Phase Lifecycle, Child Crash Recovery, Config Validation
backlog.description: Fix four issues found in creative end-to-end QA: (1) Critical — SIGTERM while armed causes double-teardown race exceeding TimeoutStopSec=15 → SIGKILL; (2) Major — phase field stays listening/speaking after disarm, never returns to idle; (3) Major — dead recorder-host child goes undetected, daemon silently stuck 'listening: on'; (4) Minor — config numeric fields accept wrong types silently, breaking auto-stop at runtime. All fixes follow TDD (implied: write failing test → implement → pass). Research findings in plan/003_27d1f88f5a9f/bugfix/001_c804c734947d/architecture/.

milestones.title: Critical Fix — SIGTERM Teardown Race (Issue 1)
milestones.description: Make the SIGTERM/systemctl-stop teardown single-flight so the armed-daemon stop completes bounded in seconds, well under TimeoutStopSec=15, with no SIGKILL. Three measures: (A) thread-safe RecorderHost.stop() via a lock so concurrent callers share ONE teardown; (B) daemon-level coordination so request_shutdown() sets the _shutdown_done guard + signals completion, letting main()'s shutdown() wait-and-return instead of starting a second concurrent teardown; (C) reduce the _bounded_shutdown timeout from 10s to 5s for headroom.

tasks.title: Make RecorderHost.stop() single-flight (thread-safe)
tasks.description: Add a threading.Lock to RecorderHost that serializes stop() calls. Concurrent callers block on the lock until the first finishes, then see self._proc is None and return immediately. This directly eliminates the 'two parallel host.stop() calls' race that is the root cause of the SIGTERM hang.

subtasks.title: Add _stop_lock to RecorderHost and wrap stop() body
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/bug_analysis.md §Issue 1 Root Cause. RecorderHost.stop() (voice_typing/recorder_host.py ~line 195) currently has NO thread-safety. Two concurrent callers both see self._proc is not None, both call proc.join(timeout) + potentially _terminate_group() + join(2). The SIGTERM path triggers this because request_shutdown() (signal thread) and shutdown() (main thread finally) call host.stop() concurrently. The existing stop() has an early guard `if self._proc is None: return` but it is NOT under a lock.
2. INPUT: The RecorderHost class in voice_typing/recorder_host.py. __init__ (~line 78) creates self._cmd_q, self._evt_q, self._abort_event, self._proc (initially None), self._reader, self._dead. The `threading` module is already imported at module top. stop() (~line 195) sends shutdown command, joins proc, optionally SIGKILLs the group via _terminate_group(), then sets self._dead=True, self._proc=None.
3. LOGIC: (a) Add `self._stop_lock = threading.Lock()` in __init__ (near the other threading primitives like self._final_evt, self._ready_evt). (b) In stop(), acquire the lock at the very start with `with self._stop_lock:` — the ENTIRE method body runs under the lock, INCLUDING the `if self._proc is None: return` early guard. This means a concurrent second caller blocks on _stop_lock until the first caller finishes (proc joined + _proc set to None), then acquires the lock, sees _proc is None, and returns immediately — no second teardown. (c) Update stop()'s docstring to note it is single-flight under _stop_lock (safe for concurrent callers; the second is a no-op). (d) Verify _STOP_JOIN_TIMEOUT_S (5.0) remains the per-call default.
4. OUTPUT: RecorderHost.stop() is safe for concurrent calls from multiple threads. Only ONE process-group teardown (join + killpg) ever executes regardless of how many threads call stop() simultaneously.
5. DOCS: none — internal implementation change, no user-facing/config/API surface change.

subtasks.title: Test concurrent stop() calls share ONE teardown
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/test_infrastructure.md. tests/test_recorder_host.py uses _make_host() to build a RecorderHost with fake callbacks, and feeds canned events via _feed_event(). The existing test_stop_with_dead_process_is_noop (~line 221) injects a fake _DeadProc into host._proc. The pattern: construct a host, set host._proc to a fake process object, call host.stop(), assert behavior.
2. INPUT: tests/test_recorder_host.py. The _make_host() helper, _FakeFeedback, _FakeLatency stubs. The real RecorderHost class (NOT _FakeHost — this tests the actual stop() lock). os.getpgid and os.killpg are called by _terminate_group() and would fail on a fake PID — they must be monkeypatched.
3. LOGIC: Create test test_concurrent_stop_calls_share_one_teardown(): (a) Build a host via _make_host(). (b) Define a _SlowProc whose is_alive() returns True and whose join(timeout) sleeps 0.3s then returns (so stop() enters the join path, not the early-return). (c) Set host._proc = _SlowProc(). (d) Monkeypatch recorder_host.os.killpg to a list-recording lambda (so it doesn't actually kill anything). (e) Monkeypatch recorder_host.os.getpgid to return a fake PID. (f) Call host.stop() from TWO threads concurrently (threading.Thread x2). (g) Assert: both threads complete within bounded time (< 2s); os.killpg was called AT MOST ONCE (not twice); host._proc is None after both finish; host._dead is True. This proves the _stop_lock serializes concurrent callers so only ONE teardown runs.
4. OUTPUT: Regression test proving RecorderHost.stop() is single-flight under concurrent access.
5. DOCS: none.

tasks.title: Coordinate daemon shutdown paths + reduce teardown timeout budget
tasks.description: Make request_shutdown() and shutdown() coordinate via _shutdown_done + _teardown_done so main()'s finally-block shutdown() does NOT start a second concurrent _bounded_shutdown(). Also reduce the default _bounded_shutdown timeout from 10s to 5s so a single teardown + server.stop() is comfortably under TimeoutStopSec=15.

subtasks.title: Single-flight coordination: request_shutdown sets _shutdown_done + _teardown_done Event; shutdown waits for in-flight teardown
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/bug_analysis.md §Issue 1 Fix Strategy. The SIGTERM race: request_shutdown() (signal thread) calls _bounded_shutdown() but does NOT set self._shutdown_done. Then main()'s finally calls daemon.shutdown(), which checks _shutdown_done (False) and calls _bounded_shutdown() AGAIN concurrently. Just setting the flag isn't enough due to timing: run() can exit (0.5s) before request_shutdown sets the flag. The fix requires BOTH the flag AND a completion signal (_teardown_done Event) so shutdown() can WAIT for the in-flight teardown rather than starting its own. Belt-and-suspenders with P1.M1.T1.S1's _stop_lock: even if both reach host.stop(), only one teardown runs.
2. INPUT: VoiceTypingDaemon in voice_typing/daemon.py. request_shutdown() (~line 1140): sets _shutdown, calls _safe_abort(), calls _bounded_shutdown(). Does NOT touch _shutdown_done. shutdown() (~line 1330): `with self._lock: if getattr(self, '_shutdown_done', False): return; self._shutdown_done = True`, then calls _bounded_shutdown(). __init__ (~line 540): creates _lock, _shutdown (Event), _shutdown_done is a getattr-guarded attribute (not set in __init__ — uses getattr default False). The threading module is imported.
3. LOGIC: (a) Add `self._teardown_done = threading.Event()` in __init__ (near _shutdown). (b) In request_shutdown(): BEFORE calling _bounded_shutdown(), acquire _lock, set self._shutdown_done = True, release _lock. AFTER _bounded_shutdown() returns (or on exception), set self._teardown_done (wrap in try/finally so it always fires). (c) In shutdown(): restructure the guard so that if _shutdown_done is ALREADY True (set by request_shutdown on another thread), it does NOT return immediately — instead it WAITS on self._teardown_done with a bounded timeout (8.0s) OUTSIDE _lock, then returns. If _teardown_done doesn't fire in 8s (e.g., the signal thread died), log a warning and proceed with _bounded_shutdown() as a fallback (safe due to the _stop_lock from T1.S1). The normal path (shutdown() called first, e.g., quit path where on_quit runs sequentially) keeps working: sets _shutdown_done, calls _bounded_shutdown(), sets _teardown_done. (d) Ensure request_shutdown() returns early (without re-tearing-down) if _shutdown_done is already True (idempotent vs a second signal or quit+signal overlap).
4. OUTPUT: On the SIGTERM path: request_shutdown (signal thread) does the ONE teardown; main()'s shutdown() waits for _teardown_done then returns — no second teardown. On the quit path: request_shutdown does teardown, shutdown() sees _teardown_done already set and returns immediately. Wall time bounded by the single teardown (~7s) + server.stop() (~2s) = ~9s max.
5. DOCS: none — internal implementation change, no user-facing/config/API surface change.

subtasks.title: Reduce _bounded_shutdown default timeout from 10s to 5s
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/bug_analysis.md §Issue 1 Fix Strategy (timeout reduction). _bounded_shutdown(self, timeout=10.0) (~line 1310) passes timeout to host.stop(timeout=timeout). Inside host.stop(): proc.join(timeout) + [if alive: _terminate_group() + join(2.0)]. With timeout=10: join(10)+join(2)=12s per call. Plus ControlServer.stop() join(2)=2s. Total=14s, dangerously close to TimeoutStopSec=15. Reducing to 5s: join(5)+join(2)=7s per call + server.stop() 2s = 9s total, comfortable headroom. _unload_host already calls _bounded_shutdown(timeout=5.0) explicitly — that is unaffected. The callers that use the default are request_shutdown() and shutdown().
2. INPUT: voice_typing/daemon.py _bounded_shutdown() method signature (~line 1310). All call sites: request_shutdown() (~line 1140, uses default), shutdown() (~line 1330, uses default), _unload_host() (~line 880, uses explicit 5.0).
3. LOGIC: Change the default parameter from `timeout: float = 10.0` to `timeout: float = 5.0`. Update the docstring budget math: 'host.stop(timeout=5) → join(5)+killpg+join(2) ≈ 7s max per call; plus ControlServer.stop() join(2) = 9s total, comfortable headroom under TimeoutStopSec=15.' Verify _unload_host's explicit timeout=5.0 call is now redundant but harmless (explicit == default). Verify no test asserts on the 10s default value.
4. OUTPUT: Each _bounded_shutdown call is bounded at ~7s. With single-flight coordination (S1), only ONE teardown ever runs, so the total is ~7s+2s=9s max — well under 15s.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the timeout-budget comment in systemd/voice-typing.service lines 42-50: change 'The 15s budget = 10s bound + 5s grace' to reflect the new 5s default (e.g., 'The 15s budget = 5s bounded teardown + 5s signal/shutdown coordination + 5s headroom'). The TimeoutStopSec=15 value itself does NOT change.

subtasks.title: Test concurrent request_shutdown + shutdown (SIGTERM path simulation) — assert single host.stop() + bounded time
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/test_infrastructure.md Run-Loop Integration Test Pattern and architecture/bug_analysis.md §Test Gap. The existing tests test_request_shutdown_sets_event_aborts_and_tears_down_child and test_shutdown_calls_recorder_shutdown_once call these methods SEQUENTIALLY on the test thread — they never exercise the CONCURRENT path (signal thread + main thread) that is the actual SIGTERM race. _FakeHost (test_daemon.py ~line 432) has stop_calls counter and is_alive property. _make_daemon() with host_factory= injects a _FakeHost.
2. INPUT: tests/test_daemon.py. _make_daemon() with host_factory=_fake_host_factory(). _FakeHost.stop_calls attribute. _wait_for() helper. threading for concurrent operations. _cuda_resolve() monkeypatch helper.
3. LOGIC: Create test test_concurrent_request_shutdown_and_shutdown_only_one_stop() that simulates the SIGTERM path: (a) _cuda_resolve(monkeypatch, ...) to avoid CUDA probing. (b) Create daemon with host_factory=_fake_host_factory() so _load_host injects a _FakeHost. (c) Start run() in thread T_run. (d) _wait_for run() booted, then call d.start() to arm (this triggers _load_host → spawns _FakeHost). (e) Simultaneously: on thread T_sig call d.request_shutdown() (simulates the signal-handler thread); almost immediately after, on the main test thread call d.shutdown() (simulates main()'s finally block). (f) Assert: host.stop_calls == 1 (NOT 2 — the fix prevents the double teardown); total wall time < 8s; the run thread exits cleanly. The _FakeHost.stop() has a brief wait (via its wrapped _StubRecorder.shutdown()), which gives the concurrency overlap window. This test directly reproduces and proves the fix for the bug the PRD describes: 'request_shutdown() runs on the signal-handler thread while main()'s daemon.shutdown() runs concurrently on the main thread'.
4. OUTPUT: Regression test for the SIGTERM double-teardown race. This is the test the PRD explicitly requests: 'Verify with a new test that drives the SIGTERM path... and asserts exit within e.g. 8s with no SIGKILL'.
5. DOCS: none.

milestones.title: Major Robustness Fixes — Phase Lifecycle (Issue 2) + Child Crash Recovery (Issue 3)
milestones.description: Fix two major state-correctness issues: (Issue 2) phase field stuck at listening/speaking after disarm — add set_phase('idle') to _disarm(); (Issue 3) dead recorder-host child goes undetected — add liveness checks in run() loop and _load_host() guard, plus _handle_dead_host() recovery method.

tasks.title: Reset phase to idle on disarm (Issue 2)
tasks.description: _disarm() never calls feedback.set_phase('idle'), so phase stays stuck at the last VAD value after any stop/toggle-off/auto-stop. Add the call after set_listening(False).

subtasks.title: Add set_phase('idle') to _disarm() to reflect 'loaded / not listening' lifecycle state
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/bug_analysis.md §Issue 2 Root Cause. _disarm() (daemon.py ~line 970) clears _listening, sets _disarmed_monotonic, calls host.set_microphone(False), calls feedback.set_listening(False). It NEVER calls feedback.set_phase('idle'). Phase is only set by VAD callbacks (on_vad_detect_start→'listening', on_vad_start→'speaking', on_vad_stop→'listening'). PRD §4.2bis: 'loaded / not listening ⇒ phase idle'. The PRD also suggests optionally gating the child's ('vad',...) relay to ignore phases while not listening (prevents a stray late VAD event from re-flipping phase after disarm).
2. INPUT: VoiceTypingDaemon._disarm() method in voice_typing/daemon.py (~line 970). Runs under self._lock (called by stop/toggle/_maybe_auto_stop). self._feedback is a Feedback instance with set_phase(str).
3. LOGIC: In _disarm(), after `self._feedback.set_listening(False)`, add `self._feedback.set_phase('idle')`. This is a one-line addition. The 'idle' phase correctly represents the 'loaded / not listening' state per PRD §4.2bis. Optional hardening: in recorder_host.py _dispatch() (~line 260), when handling kind=='vad', check if the daemon is listening before calling self._feedback.set_phase(phase) — but this requires the host to know the listening state (it does not currently). The simpler approach: accept that a stray late VAD event could transiently re-flip phase; the next disarm resets it. Only add the gate if observed in practice (PRD says 'if that is observed').
4. OUTPUT: After any disarm (stop, toggle-off, 30s auto-stop), state.json and voicectl status show phase: idle instead of the stale last VAD value.
5. DOCS: none — behavior fix, no new config/API surface.

subtasks.title: Test phase returns to idle after disarm (stop, toggle-off, auto-stop)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/test_infrastructure.md. _DaemonFakeFeedback (test_daemon.py) tracks set_phase calls via a phases list. _make_daemon() returns (d, fb, rec, be). The VAD phase test test_callback_vad_phases (~line 247) tests the FORWARD direction only (VAD events → phase). No test asserts phase AFTER disarm.
2. INPUT: tests/test_daemon.py. _make_daemon() helper. _DaemonFakeFeedback with .phases list. _make_daemon_with_feedback() for state.json tests. The VAD event can be simulated by calling d._feedback.set_phase('listening') or 'speaking' directly, or by feeding a ('vad', {'phase': 'speaking'}) event to the host's reader thread.
3. LOGIC: Create tests: (a) test_disarm_resets_phase_to_idle(): make daemon, arm (d.start()), manually set phase to 'listening' via d._feedback.set_phase('listening'), then d.stop(). Assert 'idle' is in fb.phases (the last set_phase call was 'idle'). (b) test_toggle_off_resets_phase_to_idle(): same but via d.toggle() when listening. (c) test_auto_stop_resets_phase_to_idle(): arm, set phase to 'speaking', advance time so _maybe_auto_stop fires, assert phase is 'idle'. (d) Using _make_daemon_with_feedback() with tmp_path: arm, set phase, stop, read state.json via jq/json.load, assert phase == 'idle'.
4. OUTPUT: Regression test for the phase-after-stop bug. Proves the 'loaded / not listening ⇒ phase idle' lifecycle invariant.
5. DOCS: none.

tasks.title: Detect dead recorder-host child and recover (Issue 3)
tasks.description: The run() loop never checks host.is_alive, and _load_host() short-circuits on stale _models_loaded without checking liveness. Add a liveness check in run() that detects a dead child, cleans up state via a new _handle_dead_host() method, and modifies _load_host() to re-spawn when the host is dead.

subtasks.title: Add liveness check in run() loop + _handle_dead_host() recovery method
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/bug_analysis.md §Issue 3 Root Cause. run() loop (~line 730) checks `if self._host is None` but never `host.is_alive`. When the child dies, self._host is still the dead host object, _listening is True, _models_loaded is True. host.text() returns immediately (dead child → _dead=True). After 30s auto-stop fires, _models_loaded stays True. Next start() → _load_host() short-circuits. RecorderHost.is_alive (~line 135) already exists and correctly returns False when _proc is None or not alive or _dead is True. _handle_dead_host() does not exist yet.
2. INPUT: VoiceTypingDaemon.run() (~line 700) and its while loop (~line 730). The loop structure: `while not self._shutdown.is_set(): if self._host is None: sleep(0.05); continue; if self._listening.is_set(): text(); else: sleep(0.05)`. self._lock, self._feedback (with set_phase/set_models_loaded/set_listening), self._models_loaded, self._load_error, self._listening (Event). RecorderHost.is_alive property.
3. LOGIC: (a) Add a new method _handle_dead_host() that performs cleanup under self._lock: `self._host = None`, `self._models_loaded = False`, `self._listening.clear()`, `self._feedback.set_phase('unloaded')`, `self._feedback.set_models_loaded(False)`, `self._feedback.set_listening(False)`, `self._load_error = 'recorder-host child died unexpectedly'`, `self._disarmed_monotonic = None`, `self._last_speech_monotonic = None`. Log a WARNING with the dead host's pid. (b) In run(), at the TOP of the while loop body (before the `if self._host is None` check), add: `if self._host is not None and not self._host.is_alive: logger.warning('recorder-host child (pid=%s) died; transitioning to unloaded', getattr(self._host, 'pid', '?')); self._handle_dead_host(); continue`. This detects a dead child on each ~50ms idle iteration, cleans up state, and idles. The next arm re-spawns via _load_host(). (c) The _handle_dead_host() must clear _listening (the child died WHILE listening) so status reports listening: off.
4. OUTPUT: Dead child detected within ~50ms of the next loop iteration. Daemon transitions to unloaded state (phase: unloaded, models_loaded: false, listening: false). load_error surfaces the crash in status. Next arm re-spawns.
5. DOCS: none — internal robustness fix.

subtasks.title: Add liveness check to _load_host() early-return guard
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/bug_analysis.md §Issue 3 Fix 3B. _load_host() (~line 650) has `if self._models_loaded: return True` as the first guard. When the child dies, _models_loaded stays True (never cleared until _handle_dead_host runs), so the guard short-circuits and returns True WITHOUT checking liveness — the dead host is reused.
2. INPUT: VoiceTypingDaemon._load_host() (~line 650). The early-return guard is `if self._models_loaded: return True`. RecorderHost.is_alive property exists. self._host is the current (possibly dead) host.
3. LOGIC: Change the guard from `if self._models_loaded: return True` to `if self._models_loaded and self._host is not None and self._host.is_alive: return True`. This ensures: (a) a LIVE resident host short-circuits (instant re-arm, unchanged behavior); (b) a DEAD host with stale _models_loaded does NOT short-circuit — it falls through to the spawn path, creating a fresh host. Note: in normal operation, _handle_dead_host (S1) already clears _models_loaded when it detects a dead child in run(), so by the time _load_host is called (from start/toggle), _models_loaded is False. But this guard is belt-and-suspenders for the race where _load_host is called before run() detects the death (e.g., a start() arrives between the child dying and the next run() iteration).
4. OUTPUT: _load_host() spawns a fresh host when the previous one died, instead of reusing a dead handle. Instant re-arm still works for live hosts.
5. DOCS: none.

subtasks.title: Tests: dead child detection in run(), recovery on next arm, status correctness
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/test_infrastructure.md. _FakeHost (test_daemon.py ~line 432) has is_alive property backed by self._alive flag (set True on spawn(), False on stop()). _fake_host_factory() creates a factory. Run-loop integration pattern: thread target=d.run + _wait_for(). status_snapshot() returns dict with listening, phase, models_loaded, load_error.
2. INPUT: tests/test_daemon.py. _make_daemon() with host_factory=. _FakeHost._alive can be flipped to False to simulate a dead child. _wait_for() for async assertions. status_snapshot() for status checks.
3. LOGIC: Create three tests: (a) test_run_loop_detects_dead_host_and_transitions_to_unloaded(): _cuda_resolve, make daemon with host_factory, start run() thread, arm (d.start() → _load_host spawns _FakeHost). Flip host._alive = False (simulate child crash). _wait_for(lambda: d._models_loaded is False, timeout=2). Assert: d._host is None, d._models_loaded is False, d.is_listening() is False, d._load_error contains 'died'. (b) test_load_host_respawns_after_dead_child(): arm, kill host (_alive=False), wait for cleanup, then d.start() again. Assert: a NEW _FakeHost was spawned (factory called again / spawn_calls increased), d._models_loaded is True, d.is_listening() is True. This proves recovery. (c) test_status_reports_unloaded_after_child_death(): arm, kill host, wait for cleanup. Call d.status_snapshot(). Assert: listening=False, phase='unloaded', models_loaded=False, load_error contains 'died'.
4. OUTPUT: Regression tests for the child-crash silent-failure bug. Proves detection, recovery, and status correctness.
5. DOCS: none.

milestones.title: Minor Config Hardening — Type Validation (Issue 4)
milestones.description: Config numeric fields (auto_stop_idle_seconds, auto_unload_idle_seconds, etc.) accept wrong types silently (e.g., string 'thirty' loads as str), breaking the feature at runtime when TypeError is swallowed by the watchdog. Add __post_init__ type validation to AsrConfig that raises TypeError at load time, mirroring the existing unknown-key rejection.

tasks.title: Add type validation for config numeric and string fields
tasks.description: Add __post_init__ to AsrConfig that validates field types with isinstance, raising TypeError on mismatch. This runs at construction time (when from_toml calls AsrConfig(**section)), so wrong-typed TOML values fail fast at load with a clear error.

subtasks.title: Add __post_init__ type validation to AsrConfig (and FeedbackConfig.notify_ms)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/bug_analysis.md §Issue 4 Root Cause. VoiceTypingConfig.from_toml() (config.py ~line 178) passes TOML values directly to dataclass constructors via section_cls(**section) with NO type validation. Python dataclasses do NOT enforce type annotations at runtime. So auto_stop_idle_seconds = 'thirty' (a str) loads silently. At runtime time.monotonic() - ts < 'thirty' raises TypeError, which _idle_watchdog swallows → auto-stop silently breaks. The existing unknown-key rejection uses TypeError (dataclass __init__ rejects unknown kwargs) — the new validation should use TypeError too for consistency.
2. INPUT: voice_typing/config.py AsrConfig dataclass (~line 34). Fields and expected types: final_model: str, realtime_model: str, language: str, device: str, post_speech_silence_duration: float, realtime_processing_pause: float, auto_stop_idle_seconds: float, auto_unload_idle_seconds: float. FeedbackConfig.notify_ms: int (~line 73). The dataclass decorator and field definitions. tomllib parses TOML floats as Python float, ints as int, strings as str, bools as bool.
3. LOGIC: (a) Add __post_init__ to AsrConfig that validates each field with isinstance: float fields (post_speech_silence_duration, realtime_processing_pause, auto_stop_idle_seconds, auto_unload_idle_seconds) must be isinstance(value, (int, float)) AND NOT isinstance(value, bool) — bool is a subclass of int in Python but is NOT a valid numeric config value. String fields (final_model, realtime_model, language, device) must be isinstance(value, str). Raise TypeError(f'[asr] {field_name} expects {expected_type}, got {type(value).__name__}: {value!r}') on mismatch. (b) Optionally add the same to FeedbackConfig for notify_ms (must be int, not bool, not float). (c) Ensure valid TOML values still load fine (regression guard: float 30.0, int 2500, str 'cuda' all pass).
4. OUTPUT: auto_stop_idle_seconds = 'thirty' in config.toml raises TypeError at daemon load time (before models load, before systemd Restart loops), with a clear error message naming the field and the bad value.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Add a brief comment to config.toml (~line 18-24 area, the existing 'Unknown keys are REJECTED' comment) noting that wrong-typed values are also rejected at load time (e.g., append: 'Wrong-typed values (e.g. a string where a number is expected) are also rejected with TypeError').

subtasks.title: Test wrong-typed config values raise TypeError at load
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/test_infrastructure.md. tests/test_config.py has test_unknown_key_raises showing the TypeError pattern. VoiceTypingConfig.from_toml({'asr': {...}}) is the direct construction path for unit tests.
2. INPUT: tests/test_config.py. VoiceTypingConfig.from_toml() classmethod. pytest.raises(TypeError) pattern from existing tests.
3. LOGIC: Create tests: (a) test_string_for_float_field_raises(): from_toml({'asr': {'auto_stop_idle_seconds': 'thirty'}}) raises TypeError with a message containing 'auto_stop_idle_seconds'. (b) test_bool_for_float_field_raises(): from_toml({'asr': {'post_speech_silence_duration': True}}) raises TypeError (bool is not accepted as float). (c) test_int_for_string_field_raises(): from_toml({'asr': {'device': 123}}) raises TypeError. (d) test_valid_types_still_load(): from_toml({'asr': {'auto_stop_idle_seconds': 30.0, 'post_speech_silence_duration': 0.6}}) succeeds and values are correct. (e) test_int_accepted_for_float(): from_toml({'asr': {'auto_stop_idle_seconds': 30}}) succeeds (int is acceptable for float fields — TOML allows bare integers).
4. OUTPUT: Regression test for the config type validation bug. Proves fail-fast at load for wrong types.
5. DOCS: none.

milestones.title: Changeset-Level Documentation Sync
milestones.description: Update changeset-level documentation (README.md, tests/ACCEPTANCE.md) to accurately reflect the post-fix teardown behavior, phase lifecycle, and config validation. This is the Mode B catch-all that prevents a coherent delta from shipping with stale overview docs.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation
tasks.description: Sweep README.md and tests/ACCEPTANCE.md to align teardown claims, phase lifecycle description, and config validation notes with the bug fixes. The README currently claims 'SIGKILL is gone' (inaccurate — Issue 1 proved it still happens on SIGTERM), and the phase lifecycle description does not mention the disarm→idle transition explicitly. ACCEPTANCE.md references '≤10s bounded teardown' (now ≤5s).

subtasks.title: Update README.md teardown claims + phase lifecycle description
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/system_context.md and the scout findings. README.md line 330-333 claims 'SIGKILL is gone' — this was INACCURATE before the fix (Issue 1 proved the SIGTERM path still SIGKILLed). Lines 312-316 describe phase states (unloaded/loading/idle/listening) but do not mention the disarm→idle transition explicitly. Lines 153-165 document unknown-key config rejection but not type validation.
2. INPUT: README.md. Lines ~330-333 (teardown budget), ~312-316 (phase lifecycle), ~153-165 (config validation). The fixes from P1.M1.T2 (single-flight teardown, 5s budget), P1.M2.T1 (phase→idle on disarm), P1.M3.T1 (config type validation).
3. LOGIC: (a) Lines ~330-333: Update the teardown claim from 'bounded at ≤10s' and 'The old ~90s systemd stop timeout (SIGKILL) is gone' to accurately reflect: teardown is single-flight and bounded at ≤5s per call, so systemctl stop exits in ~seconds well under the 15s TimeoutStopSec. The SIGTERM path no longer causes a double-teardown race. (b) Lines ~312-316: Add explicit mention that after disarm (stop/toggle-off/auto-stop), phase transitions to 'idle' (loaded, not listening). This documents the Issue 2 fix. (c) Lines ~153-165: Add a note that wrong-typed config values (e.g., a string where a number is expected) are also rejected at load with TypeError, mirroring the unknown-key behavior. This documents the Issue 4 fix.
4. OUTPUT: README.md accurately reflects the post-fix teardown, phase lifecycle, and config validation behavior.
5. DOCS: This IS the documentation subtask.

subtasks.title: Update tests/ACCEPTANCE.md teardown criteria + add SIGTERM-path test coverage note
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See architecture/system_context.md and scout findings. tests/ACCEPTANCE.md line ~130 references '≤10 s bounded teardown'. Line ~134 references process-group teardown (os.setsid/os.killpg). The acceptance criteria should reflect the reduced 5s budget and the new SIGTERM-path test coverage.
2. INPUT: tests/ACCEPTANCE.md. Lines ~130 (teardown budget), ~134 (process-group teardown). The fixes from P1.M1.T2 (5s budget, single-flight, concurrent request_shutdown+shutdown test).
3. LOGIC: (a) Update the teardown budget reference from '≤10 s' to '≤5 s per-call (single-flight)' to reflect the reduced timeout default. (b) Add a note that the SIGTERM/systemctl-stop path now has explicit test coverage (the concurrent request_shutdown+shutdown test from P1.M1.T2.S3) — previously only the voicectl quit path was tested, which is exactly why Issue 1 slipped through. (c) Note that child-crash recovery (Issue 3) and phase-after-disarm (Issue 2) now have regression tests.
4. OUTPUT: ACCEPTANCE.md criteria and notes match the post-fix behavior and test coverage.
5. DOCS: This IS the documentation subtask.
